---
title: "Si Lab"
---

::: {.hero}
## Spatial Ecology & Environmental Change

We study how environmental variation shapes species distributions, movement and biodiversity across space and time using quantitative ecological and geospatial methods.

We aim to understand ecological systems in a changing world and develop evidence-based strategies for biodiversity conservation and sustainable ecosystem management.
:::

---

## Research Areas

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-4}

### Movement Ecology
Satellite tracking and spatial modelling of animal movement and migration.

:::

::: {.g-col-4}

### Biodiversity Dynamics
How environmental change shapes species distributions and ecosystem structure.

:::

::: {.g-col-4}

### Global Change Ecology
Ecological responses to climate change, land-use change, and human pressures.

:::
:::

---

## Selected Highlights

- New methods for spatiotemporal ecological modelling  
- Large-scale analysis of migratory movement systems  
- Integration of long-term remote sensing and ecological data  

---

## Lab Members

Our group includes researchers working on ecology, spatial analysis, and computational modelling.

👉 See [People](people.qmd)

---

## Join the Lab

We welcome students and collaborators interested in:

- Ecological modelling  
- Movement ecology  
- Spatial data science  
- Biodiversity research
